front: What is a universal quantifier? What is its symbol?
back: A universal quantifier states that a predicate is true for every element in a specific domain. Its symbol is ∀, read as 'for all'.

front: What is an existential quantifier? What is its symbol?
back: An existential quantifier states that there exists at least one element in the domain for which the predicate is true. Its symbol is ∃, read as 'there exists'.

front: What is the domain or universe of discourse?
back: The domain, or universe of discourse, is the set from which the values of the variables in a quantified statement are chosen. It defines the scope of the quantifiers.

front: What is the negation of the universal quantifier statement ∀x P(x)?
back: The negation of ∀x P(x) is logically equivalent to ∃x ¬P(x). It means that it is not the case that P(x) is true for all x; there exists at least one x for which P(x) is false.

front: What is the negation of the existential quantifier statement ∃x P(x)?
back: The negation of ∃x P(x) is logically equivalent to ∀x ¬P(x). It means that there does not exist any x for which P(x) is true; P(x) is false for every x in the domain.
